---
title: "R vs QGIS for sustainable transport planning"
date: 2015-04-20
categories: [news]
tags: [R, QGIS, GIS, open-source]
description: "Comparing R and QGIS for transport planning"
---

Presented at GISRUK 2015 (GIS Research UK conference) on comparing R and QGIS for sustainable transport planning.

## Key points

- Both are free and open source
- R is better for analysis and reproducibility
- QGIS is better for quick visualization and editing
- Best approach: use both!

R excels at statistical analysis, reproducible workflows, and integrating with other tools. QGIS is great for interactive mapping and data management.

The best transport planning workflows often combine both.
